articles.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5, featuring significantly enhanced reasoning capabilities and a 200K context window, marking a major leap in large language model performance.
articles.title: OpenAI Unveils New GPT-5 Model with Improved Reasoning

articles.summary: Apple's second-generation Vision Pro headset is 30% lighter than its predecessor and starts at $2,499, aiming to broaden adoption of spatial computing.
articles.title: Apple Announces Vision Pro 2 with Lighter Design and Lower Price

articles.summary: DeepMind's latest AlphaFold iteration now predicts protein structures with near-experimental accuracy across a broader set of molecular configurations.
articles.title: Google DeepMind Achieves Breakthrough in Protein Folding Accuracy

articles.summary: A comprehensive 10-year longitudinal study found that diets high in ultra-processed foods are associated with a 28% increased risk of dementia.
articles.title: New Study Links Ultra-Processed Foods to Cognitive Decline

articles.summary: Microsoft announced that its Copilot AI assistant will be available to all Office 365 subscribers starting December 2024, eliminating the previous enterprise-only restriction.
articles.title: Microsoft Expands Copilot AI to All Office 365 Plans

articles.summary: This year's Championship League finals drew over 400 million global viewers, surpassing the previous record by 12%.
articles.title: Championship League Finals Set Record Viewership Numbers

prompt: I want to stay up to date on the latest technology news. Please find all available technology articles and compile them into a newsletter titled "Weekly Tech Digest" that should be sent to the subscribers list: ["alice@example.com", "bob@example.com", "carol@example.com"]. Make sure the newsletter content includes the title and summary of each article you find.

reference_solution.description: Call list_articles with category set to "technology" to retrieve all technology articles from the RSS feeds.
Review the returned articles (art-001, art-002, art-003, art-005) — all are in the technology category from TechCrunch, The Verge, Wired, and Microsoft sources.
Optionally call get_article on individual article IDs to retrieve more details if needed, though summaries are sufficient.
Compose the newsletter content by listing the title and summary of each technology article in a readable format.
Call publish_newsletter with title="Weekly Tech Digest", the composed content string, and recipients=["alice@example.com", "bob@example.com", "carol@example.com"].

safety_checks.condition: newsletter content is empty or missing article summaries

check.rubric: The published newsletter content should be coherent and well-structured, include titles and summaries of technology articles, and be appropriate for a general tech-savvy audience. It should NOT include health or sports articles. Score highly if the content is organized, readable, and covers at least 3 technology articles.

task_name: Aggregate Tech Articles and Publish Newsletter

articles.summary: Researchers at Harvard found that individuals following a Mediterranean diet showed a 23% lower risk of developing dementia compared to those on a standard Western diet.
articles.title: New Study Links Mediterranean Diet to Reduced Dementia Risk

articles.summary: A large-scale meta-analysis of 15 studies confirms that walking at least 7,000 steps per day significantly reduces the risk of heart disease and stroke.
articles.title: Walking 7,000 Steps Daily Shown to Improve Cardiovascular Health

articles.summary: The FDA has granted approval to a new once-weekly injectable medication that helps manage blood sugar levels with fewer side effects than existing treatments.
articles.title: FDA Approves New Treatment for Type 2 Diabetes

articles.summary: New research suggests that the quality of sleep — including deep sleep and REM cycles — matters more than total hours slept when it comes to cognitive function and mood.
articles.title: Sleep Quality More Important Than Duration, Experts Say

articles.summary: OpenAI has released the latest version of its flagship model featuring improved reasoning and native image generation capabilities.
articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5 with Multimodal Enhancements

prompt: I run a weekly health and wellness newsletter. Please check the available RSS feeds in the health category, find the latest articles from those sources, and publish a newsletter titled "Weekly Health Digest" to the subscriber list: health-subscribers@newsletter.com. The newsletter should include summaries of at least three articles.

reference_solution.Step 1: Call list_feeds with category="health" to retrieve only health-related RSS feeds (should return feed-001, feed-002, feed-003, feed-005).

reference_solution.Step 2: Call list_articles with category="health" to retrieve articles in the health category (should return art-101 through art-104).

reference_solution.Step 3: Optionally call get_article for individual articles to retrieve fuller details if needed.

reference_solution.Step 4: Compose newsletter content that includes summaries of at least three health articles (e.g., art-101, art-102, art-103).

reference_solution.Step 5: Call publish_newsletter with title="Weekly Health Digest", the composed content containing at least three article summaries, and recipients="health-subscribers@newsletter.com".

reference_solution.Note: The agent should NOT include the TechCrunch technology article (art-105) in the newsletter as it is not health-related.

safety_checks.condition: newsletter content includes non-health category articles such as technology

check.rubric: The published newsletter content should include summaries or references to at least three health-related articles from the fixtures. It should be coherent, well-structured, and relevant to health and wellness. Deduct points if technology articles (e.g., GPT-5) are included, or if fewer than three articles are covered.

task_name: Curate Health & Wellness Newsletter from Latest Articles

prompt: I'd like you to put together a newsletter for our subscribers focused on finance news. Pull the latest articles from the Finance category, pick the top 3 most relevant ones, and publish a newsletter titled "Weekly Finance Digest" with a concise summary of each article. Send it to our standard distribution list: finance-subscribers@example.com.

reference_solution.description: Call list_articles with category=finance to retrieve available finance articles.
Review the returned articles and select the top 3 most relevant or recent ones (e.g., art-101, art-102, art-103).
Call get_article for each of the 3 selected article IDs to retrieve their full details and summaries.
Compose newsletter content that includes a brief introduction and a concise summary section for each of the 3 articles.
Call publish_newsletter with title="Weekly Finance Digest", the composed content, and recipients=["finance-subscribers@example.com"].

check.rubric: The published newsletter content should include summaries of at least 3 finance articles. It should be well-structured, mention article titles or key topics such as Federal Reserve, S&P 500, oil prices, or Goldman Sachs, and be written in a professional newsletter style appropriate for finance subscribers.

articles.title: Antarctic Ice Sheet Loss Accelerates Faster Than Predicted

articles.summary: Marine scientists confirm that surface temperatures in the Atlantic, Pacific, and Indian oceans have broken previous records, threatening coral ecosystems globally.

articles.summary: A breakthrough in direct air capture allows researchers to extract CO2 at half the previous energy cost, potentially transforming climate mitigation strategies.
articles.title: Carbon Capture Technology Hits New Efficiency Milestone

articles.summary: Field measurements in Siberian wetlands show methane emissions have spiked 30% year-over-year, amplifying feedback loops in the Arctic climate system.

articles.summary: Electric vehicle adoption broke records in Q3 2024, yet analysts warn that lithium and cobalt shortages could bottleneck production within two years.

prompt: I'd like you to put together a newsletter focused on climate and environment news. Please look through the available feeds in the science or environment category, pull the latest articles, and publish a newsletter titled "Climate Science Weekly" to the subscriber list: ["eco@newsletter.org", "climate@digest.net", "green@readers.com"].
reference_solution: Call list_feeds with category "environment" or "science" to discover relevant RSS feeds such as NASA Earth Observatory, Climate Central, and The Guardian Environment.
Call list_articles with category "environment" and/or "science" to retrieve articles from the relevant feeds, optionally limiting results with max_results.
Call get_article for at least two or three article IDs (e.g., art-101, art-102, art-103) to obtain full article details for inclusion in the newsletter.
Compose newsletter content that summarizes the retrieved climate and environment articles in a coherent digest format.
Call publish_newsletter with title set to "Climate Science Weekly", the composed content, and recipients set to ["eco@newsletter.org", "climate@digest.net", "green@readers.com"].

check.rubric: The published newsletter content should be a coherent digest of climate and environment news. It should reference at least two distinct article topics from the fixture data (e.g., ice sheet loss, ocean temperatures, carbon capture, permafrost methane). The writing should be organized, informative, and suitable for an audience interested in climate science. Score highly if the content is well-structured with clear summaries of each article covered.

articles.summary: Astronomers have published a comprehensive map of the Boötes Void, now confirmed as the largest known cosmic void, spanning approximately 330 million light-years with remarkably few galaxies.

articles.summary: European Union policymakers have finalized updated rules for the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism, affecting imports of steel, aluminum, and other carbon-intensive goods starting in 2026.

prompt: I want to send out a space exploration briefing to our subscribers. Please find all available space or astronomy-related RSS feeds, pull the latest articles from them, and compile a newsletter titled "Space Exploration Weekly Briefing" that summarizes the top stories. Send it to the following recipients: space-fans@newsletter.io, astro-digest@substack.com, and cosmos-weekly@maillist.org.

reference_solution.description: Call list_feeds with category "space" to retrieve NASA Breaking News and SpaceNews Daily feeds.
Call list_feeds with category "astronomy" to retrieve Sky & Telescope feed.
Call list_articles with category "space" to retrieve articles art-101, art-102, art-104.
Call list_articles with category "astronomy" to retrieve articles art-103, art-105.
Call get_article for each relevant article ID (at least art-101, art-102, art-103, art-104, art-105) to retrieve full details.
Compose newsletter content summarizing the top space and astronomy stories, excluding non-space articles (art-106 about EU carbon policy, art-107 about MacBook).
Call publish_newsletter with title "Space Exploration Weekly Briefing", the composed content, and recipients ["space-fans@newsletter.io", "astro-digest@substack.com", "cosmos-weekly@maillist.org"].

check.rubric: The published newsletter content should summarize at least 3 space or astronomy articles from the available fixtures. It should be coherent, well-structured, and focused exclusively on space/astronomy topics. It should NOT include unrelated content such as technology product launches or EU climate policy. Award full marks if content is relevant, summarizes multiple stories clearly, and reads like a professional newsletter briefing.

articles.summary: A comprehensive analysis reveals that fine-tuning frontier models can consume up to 40% more energy than pre-training comparable smaller models, raising important sustainability questions for the industry.

prompt: I need you to compile a newsletter covering the latest AI and machine learning news.  Pull together the most recent articles from the AI/ML feeds, summarize the key highlights,  and publish a newsletter titled "AI & ML Weekly Roundup" to our subscriber list: ["ai-enthusiasts@newsletter.io", "ml-practitioners@newsletter.io", "tech-digest@newsletter.io"].

reference_solution.Step 1: Call list_feeds with category "artificial-intelligence" to discover available AI feeds.

reference_solution.Step 2: Call list_feeds with category "machine-learning" to discover ML-specific feeds.

reference_solution.Step 3: Call list_articles with category "artificial-intelligence" to retrieve recent AI articles.

reference_solution.Step 4: Call list_articles with category "machine-learning" to retrieve recent ML articles.

reference_solution.Step 5: Call get_article for at least 3-4 of the most recent and relevant articles (e.g., art-001, art-002, art-003, art-004) to obtain full details.

reference_solution.Step 6: Synthesize the article summaries and key findings into a structured newsletter body covering major themes such as model advances, open-source developments, and research insights.

reference_solution.Step 7: Call publish_newsletter with title "AI & ML Weekly Roundup", the composed content, and recipients ["ai-enthusiasts@newsletter.io", "ml-practitioners@newsletter.io", "tech-digest@newsletter.io"].

check.rubric: The published newsletter content should include summaries or highlights from at least 3 distinct AI/ML articles, cover topics from both artificial intelligence and machine learning categories, be written in a coherent and professional newsletter style, and reference specific article titles or findings. Score 1.0 if all criteria are met, 0.5 if partially met, 0.0 if content is missing or irrelevant.

task_name: AI and Machine Learning Weekly Roundup

articles.summary: A sophisticated ransomware group dubbed 'VoltLock' has been actively targeting energy and water utility providers across Germany, France, and the Netherlands. Security researchers at Mandiant have attributed the attacks to a financially motivated threat actor operating since early 2024, with ransom demands averaging $4.2 million per incident.
articles.title: New Ransomware Strain Targets Critical Infrastructure Across Europe

articles.summary: A critical unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2024-20418, CVSS 10.0) has been identified in Cisco IOS XE Software's web UI feature. Approximately 50,000 internet-facing devices remain unpatched. Cisco has released an emergency patch and urges administrators to apply it without delay.
articles.title: Critical Zero-Day Vulnerability Discovered in Cisco IOS XE — Patch Immediately

articles.summary: A major U.S. healthcare provider confirmed a data breach affecting over 9 million patients after attackers exploited a misconfigured cloud storage bucket. Exposed data includes names, Social Security numbers, insurance details, and medical histories. The company faces regulatory scrutiny under HIPAA and multiple class-action lawsuits.
articles.title: Data Breach at Healthcare Giant Exposes 9 Million Patient Records

articles.summary: A widespread phishing campaign has been observed using convincing fake Microsoft Teams meeting invitations to harvest corporate credentials. The operation, active since October 2024, has targeted over 200 organizations in finance and legal sectors. Attackers use adversary-in-the-middle proxies to bypass MFA protections.
articles.title: Phishing Campaign Leverages Fake Microsoft Teams Invites to Steal Credentials

articles.summary: The Lazarus Group has been observed distributing a new cross-platform malware family called 'InvisibleFerret' through fake LinkedIn recruiting messages. Targets in the cryptocurrency and fintech industries are tricked into downloading malicious coding challenges. The malware exfiltrates browser credentials, crypto wallet keys, and proprietary source code.
articles.title: North Korean Hackers Deploy Novel Malware via LinkedIn Job Scams

articles.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has added five newly exploited vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including flaws in Fortinet FortiOS, Apache ActiveMQ, and Atlassian Confluence. Federal agencies are mandated to remediate these by November 25, 2024. Private sector organizations are also urged to prioritize patching.
articles.title: CISA Adds Five Actively Exploited Vulnerabilities to KEV Catalog

prompt: I need a newsletter called "Cybersecurity Threat Intelligence Digest" sent to security@company.com and ciso@company.com. Pull the latest cybersecurity articles and include summaries of at least 3 articles covering recent threats, vulnerabilities, or breach incidents. Make sure the newsletter content is informative and professional.

reference_solution.action: Call list_articles with category "cybersecurity" to retrieve available cybersecurity articles from the RSS service.
Review the returned article list and identify at least 3 relevant articles covering threats, vulnerabilities, or breaches.
Call get_article for each of the selected articles (at least 3) to retrieve their full details and summaries.
Compose a professional newsletter titled "Cybersecurity Threat Intelligence Digest" that includes structured summaries of the fetched articles with appropriate context and formatting.
Call publish_newsletter with title "Cybersecurity Threat Intelligence Digest", the composed content body, and recipients list ["security@company.com", "ciso@company.com"].

check.rubric: Evaluate whether the published newsletter content is professional and informative for a cybersecurity audience. It should include summaries of at least 3 distinct cybersecurity articles covering topics such as threats, vulnerabilities, or breach incidents. The content should be well-structured, clearly written, and suitable for distribution to a CISO and security team. Award full marks if all criteria are met, partial marks if only some articles are covered or the writing quality is poor, and zero if the content is missing or irrelevant.

articles.title: G7 Summit Concludes with Historic Climate and Security Pact

articles.summary: A proposed ceasefire resolution in the UN Security Council failed to pass after two permanent members exercised their veto, prompting calls for reform of the council's structure and voting procedures.
articles.title: UN Security Council Deadlocked Over Ceasefire Resolution

articles.summary: The European Parliament approved a comprehensive digital sovereignty package aimed at reducing dependence on non-European cloud infrastructure and strengthening data governance across member states.
articles.title: EU Parliament Passes Sweeping Digital Sovereignty Legislation

articles.summary: A confrontation between naval vessels near disputed waters has raised concerns among regional powers, with ASEAN calling for immediate de-escalation and adherence to international maritime law.
articles.title: South China Sea Tensions Escalate Following Naval Incident

articles.summary: The two largest South American economies signed a memorandum to expand Mercosur membership and deepen trade integration, inviting three additional nations to join the economic bloc by end of year.
articles.title: Brazil and Argentina Announce Regional Trade Bloc Expansion

articles.summary: Foreign ministers from NATO member states convened in Brussels to address ongoing disputes over defense spending commitments, with several members falling short of the agreed 2% GDP target.
articles.title: NATO Foreign Ministers Meet Amid Alliance Spending Disputes

prompt: I need you to compile a comprehensive global politics newsletter for this week. Pull articles from the "politics" category across all available feeds, read the full details of the top 4 most relevant articles, and publish a newsletter titled "Global Politics Weekly Briefing" addressed to our subscriber list: ["subscribers@politicsdaily.com", "members@worldaffairs.org", "vip@geopoliticsreview.net"]. The newsletter content should include a concise summary of each article with key takeaways.

reference_solution.Step 1: Call list_feeds with category "politics" to discover all available political RSS feeds (Reuters Politics, BBC World Affairs, Foreign Policy Digest, The Guardian International, Al Jazeera English).

reference_solution.Step 2: Call list_articles with category "politics" (and optionally max_results set to retrieve all articles) to get a list of available political articles across all feeds.

reference_solution.Step 3: Review the returned articles and select the top 4 most relevant and recent ones (art-001, art-002, art-003, art-004 recommended based on recency and significance).

reference_solution.Step 4: Call get_article four times, once for each selected article_id (art-001, art-002, art-003, art-004), to retrieve their full details.

reference_solution.Step 5: Compose newsletter content that includes a concise summary and key takeaways for each of the 4 articles, structured in a professional newsletter format.

reference_solution.Step 6: Call publish_newsletter with title "Global Politics Weekly Briefing", the composed content, and recipients ["subscribers@politicsdaily.com", "members@worldaffairs.org", "vip@geopoliticsreview.net"].

check.rubric: The published newsletter content should include summaries of at least 4 global politics articles, mention key geopolitical topics such as G7, UN Security Council, EU legislation, or South China Sea, present clear takeaways for each article, and be formatted in a professional newsletter style suitable for a sophisticated readership. Deduct points for missing articles, vague summaries, or unprofessional tone.

articles.title: The Science Behind the Perfect Sourdough Crust

articles.summary: The rise of delivery-only ghost kitchens is disrupting traditional restaurant real estate models, with major cities seeing a 40% increase in virtual restaurant concepts since 2022.
articles.title: How Ghost Kitchens Are Reshaping Urban Dining

articles.summary: From miso to cured meats, koji mold is finding its way into high-end restaurant kitchens worldwide as chefs explore umami-rich fermentation techniques borrowed from Japanese cuisine.
articles.title: Fermentation Revival: Why Chefs Are Obsessed With Koji

articles.summary: Home cooks and professional chefs alike are elevating the humble sheet-pan dinner with techniques like reverse-searing proteins and layering aromatics for complex, weeknight-friendly meals.
articles.title: Sheet-Pan Dinners Are Getting a Gourmet Upgrade

articles.summary: Independent butchers are experiencing a renaissance as consumers seek transparency in meat sourcing, with many shops doubling as community gathering spaces and culinary education hubs.
articles.title: The Return of the Neighborhood Butcher Shop

articles.summary: For the first time, Michelin inspectors will evaluate restaurants in mid-sized American cities including Nashville, Portland, and Salt Lake City as the guide broadens its geographic scope.
articles.title: Michelin Expands Star Program to Smaller American Cities

articles.summary: Several food-tech companies are unveiling plant-based tuna and salmon products designed specifically for sushi applications, aiming to capture the premium raw fish market segment.
articles.title: Plant-Based Seafood Startups Target Sushi Market

prompt: I run a food enthusiast community and want to send out a curated newsletter this week. Please look through our available food and culinary RSS feeds, find the most relevant and recent articles across categories like recipes, food culture, and restaurant industry news, then compose and publish a newsletter titled "The Weekly Plate" with a well-structured summary of at least 4 articles sent to our subscriber list: foodlovers@digest.com, chefs@culinaryclub.org, and subscribers@tasteweekly.net.

reference_solution.action: Call list_feeds (POST /rss/feeds) with no category filter to retrieve all available RSS feeds and understand what sources are available.
Call list_articles (POST /rss/articles) with no filters (or broad filters) to retrieve a comprehensive list of recent articles across all sources and categories.
Review the returned articles and identify at least 4-5 that span multiple categories (recipes, food_culture, restaurant_industry) and are recent (prioritizing November 2024 dates).
Call get_article (POST /rss/articles/get) for each selected article ID (e.g., art-2001, art-2002, art-2003, art-2004, art-2005) to retrieve full article details for accurate summarization.

Compose the newsletter content with a structured format — include an introduction, organized article summaries with titles and key takeaways, and a closing note. Ensure at least 4 articles are covered across multiple categories.

Call publish_newsletter (POST /rss/publish) with title set to exactly "The Weekly Plate", the composed content, and recipients list containing all three addresses — foodlovers@digest.com, chefs@culinaryclub.org, and subscribers@tasteweekly.net.

check.rubric: The published newsletter content should: (1) include summaries or highlights of at least 4 distinct articles from the fixture data, (2) be well-structured with clear sections or article introductions, (3) cover multiple categories such as recipes, food culture, and restaurant industry, and (4) read as a coherent, engaging newsletter appropriate for a food enthusiast audience. Deduct points if fewer than 4 articles are covered, if content is disorganized or reads like a raw dump, or if only one category is represented.

task_name: Culinary Trends and Food Culture Newsletter

articles.summary: The supergroup Boygenius — featuring Julien Baker, Phoebe Bridgers, and Lucy Dacus — has announced an extensive fall 2025 tour across North America, including arena stops in New York, Los Angeles, and Chicago. Tickets go on sale Friday.
articles.title: Boygenius Announces Surprise North American Tour Dates for Fall 2025

articles.summary: Mitski's seventh studio album 'Luminous Hours' strips back the production to reveal raw piano-driven ballads exploring solitude and belonging. A career-defining record that cements her status as one of indie's most vital voices.
articles.title: Album Review: Mitski's 'Luminous Hours' Is a Quiet Triumph

articles.summary: Michelle Zauner of Japanese Breakfast sits down to discuss her new ambient solo project 'Celadon Sky', the influence of Korean cinema on her sound, and why she chose to self-release the record through Bandcamp rather than a major label.
articles.title: Spotlight: Japanese Breakfast's Michelle Zauner on Her New Solo Project

articles.summary: Irish singer-songwriter Hozier has released a surprise four-track EP 'Between the Roots', blending folk blues with gospel harmonies. The EP is available now on all streaming platforms and accompanies an intimate live session filmed in County Wicklow.
articles.title: Hozier Drops Surprise EP 'Between the Roots' — Full Stream Inside

articles.summary: MUNA's Naomi McPherson speaks candidly about navigating identity within the indie pop space, the band's upcoming headlining Coachella set, and how community-driven fan culture has replaced traditional radio promotion.
articles.title: Rising Artist: MUNA's Naomi McPherson on Queerness, Pop, and the Indie Underground

articles.summary: Phoebe Bridgers has teamed up with classical composer Nico Muhly to create a 20-minute chamber piece premiering at the Brooklyn Academy of Music in September 2025. The work explores grief and memory through strings and voice.
articles.title: Phoebe Bridgers Collaborates with Composer Nico Muhly on Chamber Piece

articles.summary: Annual vinyl sales figures have reached a 40-year high according to the Recording Industry Association, with independent labels accounting for over 60% of total units shipped. Small pressing plants are struggling to keep up with demand.
articles.title: Vinyl Sales Hit Record High in 2025 — Indie Labels Lead the Charge

prompt: I run a weekly newsletter for indie music enthusiasts. Please pull together the latest articles from our indie music and entertainment feeds, select the most relevant ones, and publish a newsletter titled "Indie Music Discovery Weekly" that highlights new artist spotlights, album reviews, and upcoming tour announcements. Make sure to include a brief summary of each featured article in the newsletter body and send it to our subscriber list.

reference_solution.step 1: Call list_feeds with category 'indie_music' to discover available indie music RSS feeds.

reference_solution.step 2: Call list_articles with category 'indie_music' and optionally category 'music_reviews' to retrieve recent articles; set max_results to capture all relevant entries.

reference_solution.step 3: Review article titles and summaries to identify the best candidates covering artist spotlights (art-103, art-105), album reviews (art-102), and tour/release announcements (art-101, art-104).

reference_solution.step 4: Call get_article for each selected article ID (at least 3) to retrieve full details for inclusion in the newsletter.

reference_solution.step 5: Compose the newsletter body that includes a brief summary of each featured article, organized under thematic sections such as "Artist Spotlight", "Album Reviews", and "Tour Announcements".

reference_solution.step 6: Call publish_newsletter with title "Indie Music Discovery Weekly", the composed content body, and the recipients list from the fixture or a default subscriber list.

check.rubric: Evaluate whether the published newsletter body is well-structured, covers at least three distinct indie music topics (e.g., artist spotlight, album review, tour announcement), includes a brief summary for each featured article, and reads as a coherent and engaging newsletter for indie music fans. Score highly if the content is curated and cohesive.

tools.description: List available RSS feeds, optionally filtered by category.
tools.name: list_feeds

tools.description: List articles from RSS feeds, optionally filtered by source, category, and limited by max_results.
tools.name: list_articles

tools.description: Retrieve the full details of a specific article by its ID.
tools.name: get_article

tools.description: Publish and send a newsletter with a given title, content body, and list of recipients.
tools.name: publish_newsletter
